title: Opalytics inference cost sensitivity discovery
preview: Initial discovery with Opalytics exploring per-token economics and hosting options. Customer provided rough traffic and tight budget constraints; discussed batching, prefix/KV caching, quantization, reserved capacity vs hosted burst, and security controls (SOC2, SSO, VPC). text: Initial discovery with Opalytics exploring per-token economics and hosting options. Customer provided rough traffic and tight budget constraints; discussed batching, prefix/KV caching, quantization, reserved capacity vs hosted burst, and security controls (SOC2, SSO, VPC). Agreed next steps: send detailed pricing worksheet, share SOC2 and network diagram, scope a 1-week perf pilot on Dedicated + hosted fallback.

title: Large Context & High TPS Backpressure Workshop - NovaFin
preview: Demo/workshop focused on handling ~100k-token contexts, sustaining high TPS while avoiding streaming backpressure and timeouts. Covered runtime behavior, KV/prefix caching strategies, batching vs latency tradeoffs, graceful fallback to smaller models, and Console/Optimize suggestions. Agreed to run a Dedicated pilot...
text: Demo/workshop focused on handling ~100k-token contexts, sustaining high TPS while avoiding streaming backpressure and timeouts. Covered runtime behavior, KV/prefix caching strategies, batching vs latency tradeoffs, graceful fallback to smaller models, and Console/Optimize suggestions. Agreed to run a Dedicated pilot and share a long-context benchmark spreadsheet.

title: Quantization regression workshop - KV aging, batching, and VPC constraints
preview: Deep technical triage of a post-quantize accuracy regression observed on Aurelia's internal clinical summarization workload. Focus areas: INT8/AWQ vs FP8 behavior, GPTQ layer sensitivity, KV cache staleness and affinity, batching-induced tokenization edge-cases, and constraints from VPC/on-prem networking. Agreed to...
text: Deep technical triage of a post-quantize accuracy regression observed on Aurelia's internal clinical summarization workload. Focus areas: INT8/AWQ vs FP8 behavior, GPTQ layer sensitivity, KV cache staleness and affinity, batching-induced tokenization edge-cases, and constraints from VPC/on-prem networking. Agreed to run targeted bisect with layer probes, share benchmark artifacts and security checklist; Redwood to provide a canary config and a small script to replay failing seeds in a private VPC test cluster.

title: Pilot Sprint 4 — latency & throughput show-and-tell (Solis Dynamics)
preview: Quick sync to review Sprint 4 benchmark results vs incumbent, validate p50/p95 deltas, discuss tail latency incidents and batching/quantization tuning. Agreed to run 24h traffic trace, try dedicated slot for short window, and share raw traces and benchmark sheet.
text: Quick sync to review Sprint 4 benchmark results vs incumbent, validate p50/p95 deltas, discuss tail latency incidents and batching/quantization tuning. Agreed to run 24h traffic trace, try dedicated slot for short window, and share raw traces and benchmark sheet.

text: Fabrikam walked through their March–May spend forecast for Redwood Hosted API and highlighted two pain points: (1) day-to-day volatility (model mix changes, retries/fallback, and caching behavior) and (2) internal budgeting requirements that prefer commit + predictable overages. Redwood shared recent metering hardening work (idempotency/dedupe on retries, billed vs raw tokens, cache-hit attribution fields, and a short shadow-billing compare period) and clarified what will and will not change in invoicing. The group discussed packaging options: staying pure PAYG with better alerting and daily exports, moving to an annual commit with discounted overage, or adding Dedicated capacity for the steady baseline and bursting to Hosted for peaks. Redwood agreed to send a proposal with two options (Hosted commit-only vs hybrid Dedicated+Hosted), plus updated guidance explaining billed tokens, ca...

title: Procurement / legal - usage definition redlines (ZenChat)
preview: ZenChat procurement/legal walked through redlines focused on (1) defining usage units (tokens/requests/embeddings), (2) retry/fallback behavior and double-charging concerns, (3) caching and whether cache hits are billed, (4) audit logs and retention/access controls for usage data, (5) invoice dispute window and proc...
text: ZenChat procurement/legal walked through redlines focused on (1) defining usage units (tokens/requests/embeddings), (2) retry/fallback behavior and double-charging concerns, (3) caching and whether cache hits are billed, (4) audit logs and retention/access controls for usage data, (5) invoice dispute window and process, and (6) credits/true-ups and how/when credits apply. Redwood agreed to send updated clause language options (token definition + retries + caching), a short billing/metering FAQ, and a proposed dispute window compromise tied to invoice preview + usage exports. ZenChat asked for evidence of audit logging and clarity on whether they can export daily usage in their preferred timezone.

title: Pricing + usage review with Acme AI
preview: Acme AI flagged a discrepancy between Feb invoice preview and their internal counts/usage exports (invoice billed tokens higher). Redwood explained the current Hosted API metering semantics (billed_tokens derived from prompt+completion tokens, model tokenizer-specific; streaming bills for tokens actually generated; ...
text: Acme AI flagged a discrepancy between Feb invoice preview and their internal counts/usage exports (invoice billed tokens higher). Redwood explained the current Hosted API metering semantics (billed_tokens derived from prompt+completion tokens, model tokenizer-specific; streaming bills for tokens actually generated; retries/fallback should not double-bill but there is a known edge case under certain server retry paths). Nadia confirmed they are rolling out improved dedupe/idempotency and cache_hit attribution fields, plus a "shadow billing compare" to validate changes before they affect invoices. The group agreed on a standard daily export schema (UTC) including request_id, idempotency_key, model, region, raw vs billed tokens, cache_hit fields, retry/fallback indicators, and status so Acme can reconcile per-request. Redwood to run a targeted reconciliation for 2026-02-01 through 2026-0...

title: StackSage onboarding feedback / activation + early retention
preview: StackSage shared candid feedback on Redwood Hosted API onboarding. They reached first value quickly using the TypeScript SDK + a minimal “hello world” snippet and streaming, but paused after initial tests because they didn’t have a clear path from “it works” to “production confidence”: evals/regression checks, cost ...
text: StackSage shared candid feedback on Redwood Hosted API onboarding. They reached first value quickly using the TypeScript SDK + a minimal “hello world” snippet and streaming, but paused after initial tests because they didn’t have a clear path from “it works” to “production confidence”: evals/regression checks, cost predictability, and a simple checklist for going from first request → 100k tokens → first feature shipped. They also noted mild friction around API key handling across environments and wanted clearer guidance on rate limits and what “good” looks like for TTFT/latency. Email onboarding was mostly helpful, but one teammate never received the second email (possible deliverability/suppression). Console nudges were useful when contextual (API key creation / first request), but they asked for less repetition after dismissal. Redwood will follow up with a short activation checklis...

title: Lifecycle growth sprint — retargeting launch checklist

Team aligned on a c...
text: Internal working session to finalize retargeting launch details for the lifecycle growth experiments sprint: audience definitions and suppression rules (active opps + enterprise domains), UTM naming conventions, landing page mapping, creative variant set, spend caps, and measurement expectations. Team aligned on a conservative ramp, a hard exclusion list refresh cadence, and how to interpret activation vs retention signals for retargeting.

title: DevFlow AI x Redwood - onboarding feedback / first request friction
preview: DevFlow AI shared onboarding friction from a recent self-serve trial of Redwood Hosted API. Main blockers were: confusion on where to create an API key + which org/project it belongs to, docs/quickstart mismatch with current SDK methods, unclear model name selection, and lack of in-console “next step” guidance after...
text: DevFlow AI shared onboarding friction from a recent self-serve trial of Redwood Hosted API. Main blockers were: confusion on where to create an API key + which org/project it belongs to, docs/quickstart mismatch with current SDK methods, unclear model name selection, and lack of in-console “next step” guidance after signup. They also hit a 401 due to using a revoked key and didn’t notice the error details; wanted a clearer error surface and copyable curl/SDK snippets in the console. Team uses Node/TypeScript and Vercel; they care about low-latency streaming for an “AI pair programmer” feature and want predictable spend. Requested: onboarding checklist in console, a single minimal “first request” path, clearer rate limit/quotas visibility, and a debugging page for common errors (401/429/model not found).

title: KV layering, lazy kernel-tiling and quantization tradeoffs — Stratus Analytics deep technical review

text: OrbitFlow wants a hybrid deployment: hosted for non-sensitive workloads and Private/VPC for PII-adjacent workloads. Team aligned on primary activation KPIs (first successful request + first 100k tokens within 7 days), experiment tracks (email drip v2, in-console nudges, paid retargeting), instrumentation/event taxonomy, and guardrails (deliverability, CAN-SPAM/GDPR, sales opp exclusions, and experiment integrity). Owners confirmed and week-by-week deliverables reviewed.

title: AWS Marketplace listing review sync - Redwood Inference (SaaS offer)
preview: AWS Marketplace team walked Redwood through SaaS listing requirements and common rejection reasons. Focus was on security documentation expectations (SOC 2/ISO posture wording, data handling, encryption/KMS, audit logs, retention) and how to present shared responsibility in the listing + attached docs. AWS recommend...
text: AWS Marketplace team walked Redwood through SaaS listing requirements and common rejection reasons. Focus was on security documentation expectations (SOC 2/ISO posture wording, data handling, encryption/KMS, audit logs, retention) and how to present shared responsibility in the listing + attached docs. AWS recommended tightening claims (avoid absolute compliance statements), ensuring support and refund language is explicit, and providing a clean architecture/data flow diagram. Timeline: AWS said first-pass review is typically ~5–10 business days after submission if artifacts are complete; security review can add a few days. Redwood targeted end-of-month submission to support a February launch window, pending final copy, screenshots, and attachment bundle.

title: Proof-of-Concept Ops Roadmap & Staffing Kickoff — Mosaic Health POC
preview: Kickoff to align PoC success criteria, dataset and access plan, sizing assumptions, benchmark targets (p50 latency < 200ms for 512 tokens, throughput of 30 req/s steady), timeline with milestones: access setup, mid-point review, exec readout. Key risks: data anonymization, SSO/SAML, network egress policy. Agreed nex...
text: Kickoff to align PoC success criteria, dataset and access plan, sizing assumptions, benchmark targets (p50 latency < 200ms for 512 tokens, throughput of 30 req/s steady), timeline with milestones: access setup, mid-point review, exec readout. Key risks: data anonymization, SSO/SAML, network egress policy. Agreed next steps and owners.

title: Cloud Marketplace Partner – Security collateral alignment (SOC 2, shared responsibility, listing requirements)
preview: AsterCloud Marketplace and Redwood aligned on the security collateral required for a marketplace listing and co-sell motion. Topics included: approved SOC 2 language and how to handle gated evidence requests (under NDA), ISO 27001 “alignment” wording (avoid certification claims), a shared responsibility model for Ho...
text: AsterCloud Marketplace and Redwood aligned on the security collateral required for a marketplace listing and co-sell motion. Topics included: approved SOC 2 language and how to handle gated evidence requests (under NDA), ISO 27001 “alignment” wording (avoid certification claims), a shared responsibility model for Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private deployments, data retention/residency language (no overpromises), and audit logging/SSO/RBAC statements suitable for the listing page. The group agreed on a review workflow: Redwood Security/GRC owns factual security claims; Product Marketing owns messaging; Partnerships owns final packaging for the marketplace submission. Action items include Redwood delivering a short “security & trust” blurb + shared responsibility table, and AsterCloud providing their listing questionnaire and required template sections.
